analysis.key_finding: sections.json was ALREADY updated to assume the tab reorder (part4 says 'The self-contradiction analysis above'; part4b says 'Why these arguments come first'), but generate-html.js tab structure was never changed. The prose and the tab order currently contradict each other. This expansion implements the tab swap to match the already-updated prose.

for_reorder: Self-contradictions use only dome-stated parameters — invulnerable to 'you're measuring from a biased frame' objection
The prose in sections.json ALREADY assumes the reorder — leaving tabs unchanged creates an 'above/below' text inconsistency
Legal brief structure (strongest first) is more persuasive than research paper structure (survey then conclude)
Dome defenders who dismiss external data encounter the self-refuting math first, removing their primary escape hatch
against_reorder: Part numbering becomes out of order (Part 4.5 appears before Part 4 in the TOC) — potentially confusing for careful readers
The sticky tab bar lets readers jump to any tab directly — claim that 'readers never reach tab 8' assumes linear reading in a tabbed interface
External falsification tests provide context (what Gaia measured, what GPS requires) that helps readers appreciate WHY self-contradictions matter
Returning readers familiar with the current structure may be disoriented by the change
both_sides_considered.verdict: The sections.json prose has already been written to assume the reorder — NOT implementing it creates a factual inconsistency ('above' referring to something below). The structural change is necessary to match the content. The Part numbering issue is minor: the TOC makes it clear, and the tab labels ('Self-Contradictions' and 'External Tests') are more important than section numbers for navigation.

changes_required.description: Swap the two tab buttons and update labels to match sections.json titles
Swap the Part 4 and Part 4.5/4.6 TOC entries so self-contradictions appear first
changes_required.old:
• Part 4: Falsification Tests
  • Part 4.5: Internal Contradictions — Does the Dome's Geometry Produce Its Claimed Predictions?
  • Part 4.6: Repository Code Analysis — What the Automation Actually Does
changes_required.new:
• Part 4.5: Self-Contradictions — The Dome's Own Geometry Refutes Its Claims
  • Part 4.6: Repository Code Analysis — What the Automation Actually Does
  • Part 4: External Falsification Tests

changes_required.description: Predictions now navigates forward to selftest (self-contradictions) instead of falsify

changes_required.location: sectionNav at end of current 'falsify' tab (line 1365)
changes_required.description: External tests tab now points forward to AI & Conclusions (it's the last of the two swapped tabs)

changes_required.location: sectionNav at end of current 'selftest' tab (line 1487)
changes_required.description: Self-contradictions tab now points forward to external tests (falsify)

Swap the entire <div class='tab-content' id='selftest'> block to appear BEFORE the <div class='tab-content' id='falsify'> block. This doesn't affect rendering (CSS shows/hides by ID) but makes source order match logical order for maintainability. The selftest div currently spans ~lines 1369-1489; the falsify div spans ~lines 1296-1367. Move the selftest block above the falsify block.

cross_references_unaffected: All existing showTab('selftest') and showTab('falsify') cross-references throughout the document (approx 20 instances) do NOT need to change. They navigate by tab ID, which is unchanged. The tab swap only changes the position of the buttons and divs, not the IDs.
part_numbering_note: Part 4.5 will now appear in the TOC before Part 4. This is a minor visual inconsistency. The sections.json titles already use descriptive names ('Self-Contradictions' and 'External Falsification Tests') which are clearer than the Part numbers. The TOC makes the hierarchy obvious. A future cleanup could renumber to Part 4 (self-contradictions) and Part 5 (external tests), but this would cascade to Parts 5→6, 6→7, 7→8 and require updating all cross-references — a much larger change that should be tracked as a separate issue if desired.
holes_addressed: ISS-453: Self-contradiction arguments (strongest) buried in tab 8 of 10
Prose/structure inconsistency: sections.json says 'above' and 'come first' but tab order puts external tests first
kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon's observation is fundamentally correct: self-contradiction arguments are categorically stronger because they use only dome-stated parameters. A dome defender cannot invoke 'biased measurements' or 'wrong frame of reference' against arguments derived from the dome's own H(r), moon altitude, and disc radius. The counter-argument (that external tests provide context, that sticky tabs allow non-linear reading) is valid but less important than the prose/structure inconsistency already present in the codebase.

anticipated_objections.objection: Part 4.5 before Part 4 in the TOC is confusing
anticipated_objections.response: The tab labels ('Self-Contradictions' and 'External Tests') are the primary navigation, not Part numbers. The TOC provides secondary navigation. The minor numbering inconsistency is less confusing than the current state where the prose says 'above' and 'come first' but the tab order contradicts it.

anticipated_objections.objection: Some readers expect external evidence before internal critique
anticipated_objections.response: The sections.json part4 intro already handles this with the framing: 'These tests provide independent confirmation... These are not needed to establish the verdict (the dome's own math already fails).' This explicitly tells readers the external tests are confirmatory, not primary.

The tab bar is always visible, and the tab names are clear. Any reader who previously bookmarked or remembered 'Internal Contradictions' will find 'Self-Contradictions' in a more prominent position. The change benefits new readers (the majority) at trivial cost to returning readers.

risk_assessment.cross_reference_breakage: LOW — all showTab() references use tab IDs which don't change
risk_assessment.sectionNav_chain: MEDIUM — three sectionNav calls must be updated simultaneously; if one is missed, navigation breaks for one tab pair
risk_assessment.build_test_coverage: The test suite checks tab anchors and section nav; it should catch any mismatches after the change
risk_assessment.docx_impact: NONE — the DOCX build uses its own section ordering in build-doc-v4.js, independent of tab order

implementation_note: This is a generate-html.js structural change, not a content change. All content is already correct in sections.json. The change is 6 edits to generate-html.js (tab bar, TOC, 3 sectionNav calls, div order). Estimated 15 minutes to implement + test. Run 'node build.js html && node test.js' to verify.
